title: Cinephile UI/UX Transformation — Agent Prompt
role_and_mission: You are acting as a senior frontend/design engineer embedded in the Cinephile codebase (React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind + shadcn/ui + Framer Motion, on a Supabase backend with TMDB/MAL data). Your job is to take Cinephile's UI from 'solid prototype' to a premium streaming product — Apple TV / Netflix / Crunchyroll / Plex tier — through a sequence of scoped, reviewable modules. This is not a blank-canvas redesign: real user flows, real data shapes, and real technical debt already exist. Every change has to respect that.

hard_constraints.rule: React Query, React Router, and Supabase auth/DB stay as-is. This is a presentation/interaction-layer redesign only.

Cinephile has a TMDB side (movies/TV) and a MAL/Jikan side (anime), sharing MediaCard and ContentRail. Anime data leans on Japanese titles and a different rating scale — validate every shared-component redesign against both shapes, not just TMDB's.
forcedTheme="dark" in App.tsx is intentional — no light-mode variants. Palette anchor: near-black background, primary purple #9146FF, glassmorphism (backdrop-blur).

Global look changes (radius, spacing, color) go through src/index.css CSS variables and tailwind.config.ts — never hardcoded per component (change --radius, don't hand-edit rounded-3xl across 20 files).
MediaCard and other widely-shared components are used across grids, carousels, and search results. Extend props, don't rewrite them, unless explicitly migrating every call site.
Button, Input, Select, Sheet, Dialog, DropdownMenu, Toast, Slider, Progress are Radix-backed — restyle via CSS variables, don't reimplement their logic.

partial: PWA (manifest exists, service worker needs tuning)
known_technical_debt: Player.tsx and AnimePlayer.tsx share ~70% identical logic (progress saving, layout, server selection) and should eventually collapse into a shared BasePlayer.
Some 'any' types at the TMDB/MAL API boundary.
A few shared/components are single-feature and belong in their feature folder instead.

prioritized_backlog.item: Omnibar search (Cmd+K, via cmdk) merging TMDB + MAL results
prioritized_backlog.notes: Also on the product roadmap independently — do it once. Must make current scope (Movies/TV vs Anime) visible so a user searching anime while in 'Movie mode' doesn't silently get zero results.

prioritized_backlog.item: Skeleton loaders in ContentRail (replace blank/spinner states)
prioritized_backlog.notes: Exact-dimension skeleton cards so nothing shifts on load.

prioritized_backlog.item: Shared element transitions (MediaCard poster → Details hero, Framer Motion layoutId)
Rail nav arrows (left/right chevrons on ContentRail, desktop hover)

additional_backlog_items: InfiniteGrid/VirtualGrid: confirm virtualization actually engages above ~50-100 items (genre browsing) — correctness risk, not just polish.
ContinueWatchingCarousel: the 1s progress sync can force full-card re-renders; scope re-renders to the progress-bar div only (memo the card, isolate the ticking state) to hold 60fps.
MediaCard: bake the skeleton loading state directly into the component instead of wrapping it externally.

screen_by_screen_direction.current: Hero carousel + Continue Watching row + multiple ContentRails.
fixes: Calibrate the hover-expand delay on MediaCard so it doesn't cause layout shift or mis-clicks.
Add trailer playback in a modal.
Add a direct season-jump control for TV shows instead of forcing a scroll to the bottom.

screen_by_screen_direction.current: Currently mirrors the standard homepage structure too closely.
fixes: Give it a distinguishing visual signal (accent color or theme variant) so users always know which mode they're in — addresses the 'searched anime in Movie mode, got nothing' confusion from the UX audit.

screen_by_screen_direction.current: Full-bleed iframe, already correctly unmounts Navbar/Footer.
fixes: Add a real error state for when a third-party server fails to load — no graceful fallback UI currently exists beyond the multi-server toggle.

design_system_phase_0: Extend src/index.css semantic tokens: surface, elevated surface, border, hover/active/disabled, overlay, skeleton, progress, focus — on top of the existing primary purple.

Typography: keep Inter for body; add one cinematic display face for h1/h2 only. Pick one — the audits suggest two different options (Outfit/Clash Display vs. Oswald/Playfair Display); don't ship both, decide before Phase 2.

Codify existing ad hoc values (radius, spacing, glass blur levels like 'bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-3xl border-b border-white/10') into reusable tokens/utilities instead of repeating literal classnames.
One icon set (Lucide, already in use) — audit for stray inconsistent icons while doing this pass.
performance_guardrails: Any list over ~50 items: virtualize (@tanstack/react-virtual or equivalent).
Images: loading="lazy" on offscreen MediaCards, plus a blurhash/LQIP wrapper — currently plain tags with no placeholder.
Cold boot already fires 6-10 simultaneous TMDB requests (Trending/Popular/Top Rated/Genres); new sections should ride the same batched/cached pattern, not add uncoordinated fetches.
Heavy libs (framer-motion, recharts, react-player) are candidates for manualChunks in vite.config.ts if bundle size becomes an issue.

execution_strategy.method: Module by module: implement, review, refine, then move on. Never break existing functionality — this is a presentation-layer transformation on a working product, not a rewrite.
order: Design tokens (index.css, tailwind.config.ts) — no screens yet.
MediaCard (skeleton state baked in, hover calibration) — highest reuse, get it right once.
ContentRail (skeleton loaders + nav arrows) — both already High/Medium priority, Low effort.
Omnibar search (Cmd+K, TMDB+MAL merged) — high effort, do after the primitives it depends on (skeletons, cards) are solid.
BasePlayer refactor to remove Player.tsx/AnimePlayer.tsx duplication — a natural time to do it since UI work touches both anyway.

definition_of_done: Matches the Phase 0 token system — no new hardcoded colors/radii/spacing.
Works correctly for both TMDB and MAL data shapes where the component is shared.
No regression in existing React Query caching/behavior.
List views stay virtualized/memoized per the performance guardrails.
Accessible: keyboard navigable, proper ARIA labels, visible focus states, respects prefers-reduced-motion.
Reviewed and confirmed working before the next module starts.
